data.title: SolarEdge: I Was So Wrong, Sell This Rally (Double Rating Downgrade)
data.description:
SolarEdge Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ:SEDG) was soaring on Wednesday as investors appeared optimistic that the worst was behind the company. The company achieved positive free cash flow in the quarter and management has guided for positive free cash flow for the 2025 year. Despite having substantial losses on my long position, I am now throwing in the towel as I am now of the view that I have underestimated the competitive threats to this business. Today’s rally looks like a welcome opportunity to close my position, as I am concerned about the profitability outlook for the company. I am downgrading the stock to a sell rating.

SEDG Stock Price

I last covered SEDG in March 2024 where I rated the stock a buy due to their aggressive share repurchase program. The stock is down a staggering 65% since then, as compared to a positive 18% return from the broader market.

I don’t like having to sell at a loss — let alone a deep loss — but I was wrong here, and I don’t see reason for stubbornness. I had hoped that the negative gross margins seen in that first quarter of 2024 would prove transitory, but I was very wrong.

SEDG Stock Key Metrics

SEDG sells microinverters which are used to help maximize energy conversion across solar energy systems. A couple of years ago, investors (including yours truly) were predominantly of the view that SEDG and Enphase (ENPH) operated under a fruitful duopoly in the sector. Nowadays, Tesla (TSLA) looks like a leading disruptor, with ENPH being the other leader.

SEDG closed out 2024 with $196 million in revenue, representing a 38% YOY decline.

Unlike ENPH, which has managed to maintain solid gross margins and overall profitability, SEDG has seen its gross margins deteriorate alongside plunging sales. Gross margin came in at negative 57.2% in the quarter and negative 97.3% for the full year.

After including operating expenses, the company lost $263.7 million in operating losses in the quarter and $1.7 billion in the full year.

On the bright side, the company finally returned to positive free cash flow “generation,” with $25.5 million of free cash flow in the quarter. Management has guided for positive free cash flow for 2025. I put “generation” in quotes because with the company still being solidly unprofitable on an operational basis, this cash flow is mainly coming from other levers like inventory management. SEDG ended the quarter with $628.5 million of cash versus $676 million of debt and $432 million of warranty obligations. This company once had a solid net cash position, but the brutal solar winter has taken its toll on the balance sheet. I note that roughly half of the debt position is set to mature later this year. On the conference call, management reiterated expectations to redeem the maturing notes using cash on hand. I find this likely given that these notes carry a 0% interest rate, and it would presumably be difficult to refinance them at attractive rates, given the company’s current financial position.

Looking ahead, management has guided for the first quarter to see up to $215 million in revenue, representing a return to mid-single-digit growth and non-GAAP gross margins to come in at up to 10%. These aren’t great numbers, but they aren’t as bad as they have been over the past year, so perhaps investors are searching for reasons for optimism.

Management noted that they recorded $115 million in inventory impairments in the quarter due to weakness in the European markets. This was the second consecutive quarter with inventory impairments. Management also noted that they have revised $25.5 million of previously reported revenues and loan receivables with a struggling customer. An analyst also asked what appeared to be a potential “softball” question regarding incremental gross margins on U.S. residential inverters after excluding the “fixed COGS that have held back the margins at these lower revenue levels.” Surprisingly, management chose not to offer additional disclosure there. Quite frankly, I am surprised by the stock’s large jump today and suspect that it might have something to do with the 39% short interest.

Is SEDG Stock A Buy, Sell, Or Hold?

Consensus estimates look optimistic, as $1.2 billion in projected 2025 revenue does not look reasonable given the first quarter outlook. Yet, the stock does trade undeniably cheap at just around 1x forward sales. In comparison, ENPH trades at around 5.6x sales.

The problem is that whereas ENPH remains profitable on a GAAP basis, SEDG continues to operate at deep GAAP losses. The P/S valuation only makes sense if SEDG can eventually return to profitability, but analysts do not expect a recovery until 2026.

At this point, it is rather clear that SEDG might not be able to get out of this predicament through execution. The company has historically competed at a lower price point, and that is clearly evident in the “night and day” margin profiles as compared with ENPH. It is now looking like SEDG will need a plunge in interest rates (and associated increase in solar demand) before it can see a recovery in margins and growth rates. Even then, would it be able to recapture the market share lost to ENPH, TSLA, and others? With inflation still stubbornly persistent, I find it unreliable to bank on a fall in interest rates and believe that investors may be underestimating the risk to SEDG’s balance sheet.

SEDG Stock Conclusion

SEDG investors have been waiting for a turnaround for a long time, and the stock’s rally today seems to indicate that some believe that this has finally come. I unfortunately am skeptical and view today’s rally as an opportunity to cut losses and move on. While management is guiding for positive free cash flow this year, I continue to expect deep GAAP losses. I expect SEDG to continue to struggle in the current high interest rate environment as it loses market share to formidable competitors. I am downgrading SEDG to a sell rating.

data.title: Fiverr International Stock Selloff Is An Opportunity
data.description:
Today we are looking at the stock of Fiverr International Ltd. (NYSE:FVRR) which is selling off on its earnings report. In our opinion, this is a buying opportunity. For our followers who are unfamiliar, this is a service that connects freelancers with people who need a service or a particular skill. For example, maybe you want a logo for your new business, or perhaps you want someone to make a musical jingle for your podcast. Maybe you require someone to build a simple website. You can go to Fiverr and find people willing to do this work with up front contract pricing. It is like a giant online talent pool. So people come and connect with freelancers offering their services, and Fiverr gets a cut. That is the business model.

Over time, it has grown to have many of thousands of participants and customers on the platform, and along the way, sales have grown, as have earnings power. And they are still in growth mode, and are evolving and innovating. For example, they just launched a new AI platform for its freelancers. This will help freelancers reach new customers and protect their work, at least, that is how it is built. Further, they are offering equity to top freelancers, which could attract more content creators for growth. And on a valuation basis, this stock is really cheap. Further, the outlook for the year was above consensus. While the quarter itself was a bit of a miss, we think this selloff is a buying opportunity. Let us discuss the just reported quarter.

So the just reported quarter once again showed growth. The top line came in at $103.7 million, compared to $91.5 million a year ago. This was an increase of 13.3% year-over-year. This was a beat against consensus estimates of $2.3 million, which was quite positive. What went into this? Most of the revenue comes from the marketplace, where people are buying services. There was a 4% dip here, which may have caught some by surprise. The marketplace revenue was $73.5 million, down 4.0% compared to $76.6 last year. This was due to a smaller customer count, and this may in part be contributing to today's selling. Annual active buyers were 3.6 million at the end of 2024, 10% lower than 4.0 million at the end of 2023. But average spending of these customers is up. The average spends per buyer hit $302, compared to $278 last year, 9% higher. And, the company is taking a bigger piece of the pie, with a take rate up 20 basis points to 27.6%. We view this as quite positive overall. Where real gains are being has is in services, where revenue more than doubled to $30.2 million, versus $14.9 million last year.

Expenses are on watch here because we have seen some minor margin compression. But still, an 80% plus margin is strong. As reported, gross margins dipped 260 basis points to 80.5%. Making adjustments for one-time expenses, gross margins were 84.0%, a decrease of 60 basis points compared to a year ago. Adjusted EBITDA grew to $20.7 million, compared to $16.1 million last year, while Adjusted EBITDA margin expanded to 20.0% from 17.4% last year. Factoring in operating expenses, net income was $12.8 million, or $0.33 per share, compared to or $0.12 last year. That is positive. On an adjusted basis, we saw $0.64 up from $0.56 last year. However, that did miss consensus by $0.04.

From a cash flow perspective we are also seeing improvement. The net cash from operating activities grew 9% to $30.0 million, versus $27.5 million a year ago while free cash flow was up 8.1% to $29.6 million. We think it is also worth noting the entire year saw $2.38 in EPS up from $1.95 last year, which is strong growth. And for the year, free cash flow was also up 14.3% to $93.9 million.

All of this is positive news. Even if Q4 came up a little short. The reason we think this selloff is an opportunity at this valuation, at $30 per share, is the 2025 outlook was well above consensus. Initially, shares were up on the news, and are now down over 8.5% at the time of this writing, which seems punitive on a positive outlook above consensus. The consensus for Q1 revenue was $102 million, but the guide is above this even at the low end. Management sees $103.5-$108.5 million in revenue, while for the year, the guide of $422.0 to $438 million is very bullish relative to the $418.6 million consensus. On this alone it is almost a buy given the forward price to sales of 2X. But The EBITDA outlook of $82-$90 million is also positive, relative to the $74.2 million in 2024. On a per-share front, assuming comparable margins to 2024, we see EPS hitting 2.64 to $2.90. This is $2.77 at the midpoint. Giving us a really attractive 10.8X FWD valuation.

With $133 million in cash and equivalents and positive free cash flow, the debt of about $450 million long-term is very manageable. All things considered, we view this as a buying opportunity.

data.title: BNY Mellon International Equity Fund Q4 2024 Commentary
data.description:
“While certain stocks have been negatively affected by the protectionist rhetoric emanating from the incoming Trump administration, as well as other geopolitical developments in Europe, we believe this can present attractive opportunities to invest in businesses where we have conviction in long-term prospects.”

Performance Summary

Weighed down by a strengthening US dollar, international equities declined over the quarter in base-currency (US dollar) terms. Digesting largely resilient economic data and the latest comments from officials on the trajectory of interest-rate cuts, the start of the review period saw investors rein in expectations that the US Federal Reserve would cut interest rates aggressively at its next policy meeting. Meanwhile, the initial enthusiasm that had welcomed China’s stimulus program waned as investors awaited greater clarity. In the UK, investors reacted to details of the Labour Party’s first Budget and the significant package of tax rises and borrowing it entailed. Elsewhere, a period of political uncertainty beckoned in Japan as Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba’s Liberal Democratic Party lost its parliamentary majority. By contrast, Donald Trump’s decisive victory in the US presidential election saw investors adopt the view that US exceptionalism, both from an economic and market perspective, would continue under a new administration. Outside of the US, however, the election received a far more cautious response. In particular, President-elect Trump's threats to impose duties on imports from Europe weighed on the region’s equity markets. Increased geopolitical uncertainty, as Russia-Ukraine tensions ramped up once again, combined with political turmoil in France, Germany and South Korea, was also unhelpful. International equities continued to weaken over the final stages of 2024. Although the US Federal Reserve trimmed interest rates in December, a more cautious outlook for rate cuts in 2025 surprised investors, rattling equity markets towards the end of the year. Fresh stimulus headlines from China were initially received positively, yet a lack of concrete detail around potential measures to boost domestic consumption disappointed once again. Meanwhile, investors continued to fret about President-elect Trump’s protectionist rhetoric.

BNY Mellon International Equity Fund outperformed its benchmark index, the MSCI EAFE (“the index”), during the fourth quarter of 2024.

The Fund fell but outperformed over the quarter, finishing the year comfortably ahead of the performance benchmark. Relative returns were boosted by strong stock selection in financials, materials and consumer discretionary. Overweight positioning in financials also proved beneficial as the incoming Trump administration presented the prospect of a looser regulatory environment in the US. On the other hand, negative stock-selection effects materialized in information technology, industrials, health care and utilities.

Top Contributors

Continuing to execute against its three-year plan laid out earlier in the year, Barclays delivered pre-tax profits above expectations for the third quarter, boosted by higher net interest income and encouraging results from the investment bank division. Apparently well-positioned to benefit from an improved fundamental and regulatory backdrop in the US under the Trump administration, Barclays’ shares continued to perform positively.

Standard Chartered (OTCPK:SCBFF)(OTCPK:SCBFY) reported pre-tax income ahead of expectations for the third quarter, boosted by a record period for its wealth business. The bank raised its capital distribution outlook, revenue guidance and return on tangible equity target, a key measure of profitability. Management is taking action to simplify the business, which we believe should help to generate high-quality growth and improve profitability over the medium term.

Heidelberg Materials (OTCPK:HDLMY) performed strongly following robust results for the third quarter as sales and earnings registered above consensus. The announcement of a new self-help plan, named ‘Transformation Accelerator’, was received positively by investors.

Shares in Novo Nordisk (NVO)(OTCPK:NONOF) declined as its next-generation obesity drug CagriSema disappointed in a late-stage trial. Consequently, the fund’s underweight positioning in this sizeable index constituent proved beneficial.

Top Detractors

Andritz (OTCPK:ADRZF)(OTCPK:ADRZY) reported a mixed set of quarterly results as sales underwhelmed. Downbeat management commentary around the current demand environment for the company’s end markets called into question the prospects of a near-term recovery.

UK housebuilder British Land (OTCPK:BTLCY)(OTCPK:BRLAF) retreated as investors reacted to the possibility of a harsher rate environment following the government’s Budget announcement.

Pressured by rising bund yields and political uncertainty in Germany, shares in utility business E.ON (OTCPK:EONGY)(OTCPK:ENAKF) declined over the quarter. However, we continue to like company fundamentals despite the short-term vagaries of the political backdrop.

Amid continued concern around its competitiveness in high-bandwidth memory chips, Samsung Electronics (OTCPK:SSNLF) struggled. Although news of a share buyback was welcomed by investors during the quarter, a reshuffling of management in its chip division saw a muted market response. Renewed US efforts to prevent China from advancing its chipmaking capabilities also weighed.

Market Outlook

Investor worries around the US labor market have ebbed in recent months, while concerns about stalling progress in the fight to bring inflation down have returned. Indeed, a key area of focus is likely to remain the outlook for interest-rate cuts in 2025. Expectations have grown increasingly cautious of late, particularly given the potential inflationary impact of President-elect Trump’s apparent desire to impose tariffs, implement tax cuts and reduce regulation. As markets consider the pace of interest-rate cuts and the rhetoric around any given move, labor markets, inflation and growth numbers look set to remain front of mind. Meanwhile, market participants have seemingly adopted the view that a period of US exceptionalism, both from an economic and market perspective, is likely to continue under a Trump administration. US equity market valuations are higher than those in other regions, and while the outperformance of US stocks may continue, its recent magnitude could prove hard to match in 2025. The positive performance of technology companies has dominated the market, yet there have been signs over 2024 that the artificial intelligence (AI) theme is coming under closer scrutiny. While certain stocks have been negatively affected by the protectionist rhetoric emanating from the incoming Trump administration, as well as other geopolitical developments in Europe, we believe this can present attractive opportunities to invest in businesses where we have conviction in long-term prospects.

Investors should consider the investment objectives, risks, charges and expenses of a mutual fund carefully before investing. Past performance is no guarantee of future results.

Risks

Diversification cannot assure a profit or protect against loss. Equities are subject to market, market sector, market liquidity, issuer, and investment style risks, to varying degrees. Currencies are subject to the risk that those currencies will decline in value relative to a local currency, or, in the case of hedged positions, that the local currency will decline relative to the currency being hedged. Each of these risks could increase the fund's volatility. Investing in foreign denominated and/or domiciled securities involves special risks, including changes in currency exchange rates, political, economic, and social instability, limited company information, differing auditing and legal standards, and less market liquidity. These risks generally are greater with emerging market countries.

Index Definitions

The Morgan Stanley Capital International Europe, Australasia, Far East (MSCI EAFE®) Index is a free float-adjusted market capitalization weighted index that is designed to measure equity performance in developed markets excluding the United States and Canada. The index consists of select designated MSCI national developed market indices. Investors cannot invest directly in any index.

Definitions

Q is quarter. NAV is Net Asset Value. YTD is Year to Date. FDIC is Federal Deposit Insurance Corp. Tangible equity target, or tangible common equity (TCE), is calculated by subtracting intangible assets (including goodwill) and preferred equity from the company's book value.

As of 12/31/24 the companies mentioned represented 13.36% of the fund’s portfolio in the aggregate. The holdings listed should not be considered recommendations to buy or sell a particular security. data.title: Enbridge Has Continued Growth Potential
data.description:
Enbridge (NYSE:ENB) is one of the largest midstream companies in the world, worth almost $100 billion. The company has continued to invest massively within its business, and in addition, has even further growth potential. Despite concerns around the balance sheet, the company continues to pay a dividend of more than 6% and has the ability to generate strong shareholder returns.

Overall, that makes the company a valuable investment, with respectable growth since the last time we discussed it.

Enbridge 2024

Enbridge had a strong 2024 with substantial double-digit growth in adjusted EBITDA.

The company managed strong shareholder returns in 2024. At the same time, the company managed its 30 consecutive years of growing dividends. The company's growth was supported by its $19 billion (CAD) acquisition of three premier U.S. natural gas utilities, and the company has continued to sanction new organic growth.

The company has taken advantage of a number of growth opportunities and debt-to-EBITDA remains within its target range.

The company has managed to continue growing its DCF/share in a tough market environment. The company hit its financial metrics for 19 years in a row, and it has incredibly predictable cash flows. The company's business is centered around liquids and gas, along with a strong renewable power business as well. The company has long-term PPAs and a utility-like model.

Enbridge Segment Performance

Enbridge has seen strong performance across its segments.

The company's liquids business remains one of the most impressive, with its Mainline north-to-south pipeline arguably one of the most important in the world. The company saw more than 3 million barrels/day in throughput with double-digit ROE earnings. The company is continuing to look for additional expansion opportunities and has sanctioned Permian franchise expansions.

The company is building up a massive gas business, taking advantage of distribution to customer opportunities and transmission. The company sanctioned $4 billion in new projects, with more than $1 billion of capital in service, and a natural gas presence in the massive Permian Basin. The company has more than 7 million gas delivery customers.

The company's renewable energy business saw gigawatts of major solar projects put into service. These projects are backed by long-term PPA agreements with a number of companies. The company sanctioned $1.8 billion in solar projects with <2-year construction windows, which will provide strong improvements to its utility-like business model.

Enbridge Financial Results

The company's financial results show its continued strong cash flow.

The company had $3.92 USD DCF in 2024, up 5% YoY. The company's DCF per share growth was smaller as the company's outstanding share count increased. The company earned $18.6 billion in adjusted EBITDA for the year ($13.1 billion USD). The vast majority of this comes from the company's liquids pipelines business.

The company's maintenance capital remains minimal and given the debt load financing costs remain lofty at $3.5 billion in annual interest (USD). That's a 9% single-digit DCF yield, a higher valuation, than where the company used to sit, but still a valuation where there's substantial opportunity.

Enbridge 2025

Enbridge is focused on achieving strong growth going into 2025.

The company expects to see double-digit adjusted EBITDA growth moving to $5.7 DCF/share at the midpoint. That's a reasonable YoY improvement (2%) but it shows how the company continues to be impacted by rising interest rates for rolling over debt in 2025. Still, that'll push the company past $4 USD/share.

The company's net goal here is to continue investing within its growth while maintaining its dividend aristocrat status. The company paid $8 billion CAD of dividends in 2024, and it maintains a more than 6% dividend yield. That's an impressive dividend yield that the company can comfortably afford to continue paying.

The company is continuing to achieve utility-like growth and opportunities while extending its backlog opportunities. The company's continued investment in its business, on top of a commitment to strong shareholder returns, indicates it's a valuable investment.

Thesis Risk

The largest risk to our thesis is the company's 4.5x-5.0x leverage on $14 billion USD of adjusted EBITDA. That's a debt load that goes towards $70 billion USD, one that costs the company billions annually. It's a massive cost for the company and one that could hurt the company substantially in the event of a downturn.

Conclusion

Enbridge has an impressive portfolio of assets, worth almost $100 billion. The company pays out a dividend of more than 6% that it can comfortably afford with its dividend aristocrat status. The company is building up its utility-like businesses with growth in gas distribution and renewable energy. That is providing the company with reliable cash flow.

The company is continuing to invest billions annually. While its leverage is manageable, we'd like to see it not expand at all from its already high range, and stay closer to the 4.5x level. That could enable the company to drive valuable shareholder returns, making it a strong investment opportunity.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: Ionis Pharmaceuticals Reports Strong Q4 Earnings After Recent FDA Approval
data.description:
Ionis Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:IONS) is a Carlsbad, CA based biopharma specializing in RNA-targeted therapeutics. In December, the FDA approved TRYNGOLZA (olezarsen) as an adjunct to diet for the reduction of triglycerides in adults suffering from familial chylomicronemia syndrome (FCS). FCS is a rare genetic disorder leading to dangerously high triglyceride levels and eventually life-threatening complications, such as pancreatitis. TRYNGOLZA is the company's first independent commercial launch, where previously it has had big pharma partners for commercialization efforts. The US market for the rare disorder FCS is estimated to be approximately 3,000 patients.

On Wednesday, Ionis reported Q4 earnings and released guidance for 2025. The results beat the consensus on the top and bottom line. GAAP EPS for Q4 2024 came in at -$0.66/share, a beat of $0.47. Revenue of $227M (-30.2% YoY) beat expectations by $86.1M. Revenue guidance for 2025 was >$600M, versus a consensus estimate of $677M. Despite somewhat lower than expected guidance, the report was well received and the stock has traded higher.

Ionis is well capitalized, with $2.3B in cash, cash equivalents and short-term investments as of the end of the fourth quarter. Due to the company's >$5B market capitalization, TRYNGOLZA's approval to date may be insufficient for the company to reach profitability. In 2025, the major catalyst for shares will be 3 pivotal Phase 3 trials on TRYNGOLZA in severe hypertriglyceridemia (sHTG) a much more common condition with >3 million patients. The company is then expecting to file an sNDA by year end, a major bullish catalyst if successful.

Ionis has a robust pipeline containing over 40 RNA-targeted therapeutics, a massive number given the firm's market capitalization. Investors eager for exposure to RNA-therapeutics should be attracted by Ionis' recent execution. At the present time, I rate the company a HOLD, maintaining Ionis on my watch-list until a catalyst emerges to propel the company to profitability. That catalyst may be label expansion of TRYNGOLZA in sHTG by year end, thus Ionis is a compelling stock to watch in the coming year.

Ionis Pharma: Q4 Earnings Highlights

On the Q4 2024 earning call, CEO Brett Monia began by showcasing Ionis' success in the FDA approval of TRYNGOLZA. Following FDA approval, FCS patients were dosed within 2 weeks, cementing the company's first mover advantage. TRYNGOLZA is Ionis' first wholly owned commercialized product, with previous drugs WAINUA partnered with AstraZeneca (NYSE:AZN) and SPINRAZA partnered with Biogen (NASDAQ:BIIB).

In FCS, approximately 3,000 patients are diagnosed, with the majority remaining undiagnosed. Thus, Ionis may face some barriers to finding patients as TRYNGOLZA continues its launch. This rare genetic disorder may not have the immediate commercialization potential required to bring the company to profitability.

However, Ionis is moving to expand the label into sHTG, with 3 Phase 3 trials (ESSENCE, CORE & CORE-2). Enrollment in all three studies is complete and results are expected by the end of 2025. sHTG is a far more common condition, with an estimated >3 million patients in the US. Additionally, under-served patients may suffer from acute and potentially fatal pancreatitis. Given the drug's success in FCS, expansion into sHTG seems like a strong strategic direction. Positive results would prompt Ionis to file a supplementary new drug application ((sNDA)) by the end of 2025. The most advanced study, named ESSENCE is expected to report safety study data by mid-2025.

In 2024, Ionis achieved $705M in revenue, with $293M coming from commercial revenue and $412M arising from R&D revenue. The majority of commercial revenue was from SPINRAZA, with a new royalty stream coming from WAINUA. The company spent heavily on R&D, with R&D expenses contributing to $810M of total operating expenses, leading to an operating loss of $345M in 2024. The substantial investment in R&D (~16% of market capitalization) represents a compelling rationale for investment in the company.

While guidance for 2025 did not break down sales by product, revenue is seen coming slightly below 2024 (>$600M), leading to a widening operating loss of <$495M and reducing cash to ~$1.7B.

At the present time, TRYNGOLZA's promise in sHTG is substantial, however, the company still requires a wholly owned blockbuster in order to justify its present market capitalization. Investors should closely watch for continued execution of TRYNGOLZA in sHTG.

Ionis Pharma: The Path to Profitability

Analyst's see revenue growth accelerating over the next few years, growing to $1.76B by 2028. The approximately ~$1B in additional revenue reflects confidence that Ionis' pipeline will present investors with a blockbuster commercialization in the coming few years.

Should this occur, Ionis is poised to reach an inflection to positive cash flow and earnings by 2028. The potential launch of TRYNGOLZA in sHTG may be the catalyst that the market requires. BMO Capital projected $341 million in peak sales for TRYNGOLZA in FCS, but expected "meaningfully larger opportunity" in sHTG of "five or six times higher than in FCS." Thus, analysts believe that the sHTG opportunity could be as high as $2.05B annually.

Should this transpire, sales of TRYNGOLZA in sHTG would begin in 2026, growing to meet analyst's expectations for an earnings inflection by 2028. Thus, a positive outcome in sHTG would be very material and could propel the stock higher by the end of 2025.

Conclusion

There is much for investors to celebrate in Ionis' Q4 earnings report, especially the company's first fully-owned commercialization. However, the opportunity for TRYNGOLZA in FCS, along with the partnered drugs WAINUA and SPINRAZA may still be insufficient for the company to outperform in the coming year. Instead, the coming catalyst of expansion into sHTG represents the inflection point that may power Ionis to achieve positive earnings and cashflow. Success in sHTG would also transform the company into a very attractive takeover candidate.

As such, I believe Ionis is best positioned as a watch-list stock. I prefer a wait and see approach as Ionis' commercialization of TRYNGOLZA unfolds, rating the stock a HOLD at the present time. I expect that should the expansion into sHTG unfold in an optimistic light, ~$2B in annual sales could justify the stock doubling to reach a market capitalization of $10B. Investors today may wish to take that bet given the strong execution of the company, but should be aware that it will take a minimum of 1-2 years for the sHTG commercialization roll-out.

data.title: BNY Mellon International Bond Fund Q4 2024 Commentary
data.description:
“We see a positive, though sub-trend growth environment, with inflation remaining above target and falling only slowly. The beneficial base effects helping to push inflation lower have faded, introducing a greater likelihood of higher headline rates in coming months.”

Market Review

Government bond markets were notably weaker at the end of the fourth quarter, as the economic backdrop continued to improve, though accompanied by increased concerns that further declines in inflation may be harder won, with greater risks that it could experience a spike higher. The potential effects of expansionary fiscal policies in a number of areas have added to the increased yield levels. Credit markets were widely stronger than government markets in the quarter, as spread levels tightened overall, although both the US investment grade and high yield markets underperformed their European counterparts.

Performance Summary

For the quarter ended December 31, 2024, the Fund’s Class I shares returned -6.51%, excluding sales charges. In comparison, the Fund’s unmanaged benchmark, the Bloomberg Global Aggregate USD Hedged Index, returned -6.84% for the same period.

Returns are net of fund expenses and assume reinvestment of distributions. The performance data quoted represents past performance, which is no guarantee of future results. Share price and investment return fluctuate, and an investor's shares may be worth more or less than original cost upon redemption. Current performance may be lower or higher than the performance quoted. Performance for periods less than 1 year is not annualized. Go to BNY Mellon Investment Management - Global Home - English for the fund's most recent month-end returns. Returns assume the reinvestment of dividends and capital gains, if any.

Total Expenses (12/31/24)

Gross 1

Net 2

Class A

1.10%

Class I

0.77%

0.77%
1 Gross expenses is the total annual operating expense ratio for the fund, before any fee waivers or expense reimbursements. 2 Net Expenses is the total annual operating expense ratio for the fund, after any applicable fee waivers or expense reimbursements. The net expense ratio(s) reflect a contractual expense reduction agreement through 3/1/2025, without which, the returns would have been lower. The Net Expenses is the actual fund expense ratio applicable to investors. Not all classes of shares may be available to all investors or through all broker-dealer platforms.

Despite headline inflation increasing to 2.7% in the period, the Federal Reserve (Fed) cut interest rates twice, taking the Fed funds rate to the 4.25%-4.50% range. At the same time, policymakers have become more hawkish and now envisage fewer and/or a slower pace of rate cuts ahead, with potentially as few as just two further cuts in 2025. The change of rhetoric helped keep the US dollar underpinned. The Institute of Supply Management (ISM) Manufacturing Purchasing Managers Index (PMI) also recovered from its late-summer malaise, which has led many market participants to suggest the soft landing has occurred and an improved outlook is ahead. Not all forward-looking indicators have been improving though. The Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing Index declined sharply in its last two reports after previously rising, reaching -16.8 in December, its lowest level of 2024. Beyond the economic data and Fed activity, the markets were focused on the US elections in November. Donald Trump’s and the Republican Party’s victories increased expectations that a mix of expansionary fiscal policy and trade tariffs will present markets with plenty of potential challenges, gauging the future path of growth and inflation.

The European Central Bank (ECB) cut rates twice in the quarter, by 25bp each time. Inflation initially slumped below the central bank’s target rate of 2% but rose again later in the period. GDP growth for the euro region was revised lower, to just 0.2% in the third quarter, with the modest expansion led primarily by peripheral nations.

German growth has remained sluggish, with the country registering negative growth in each of the last four quarters. Forward-looking indicators have been volatile. The ZEW Economic Sentiment Index first rose, then fell back before rising again at the end of the year. However, at 17.0, it remains below levels recorded for most of 2024. Political turmoil has been a feature of core Europe, and France particularly, during the period, with President Macron’s initial appointment of Michel Barnier as Prime Minister, having to resign after losing a no- confidence vote. His replacement as Prime Minister, Francois Bayrou is hoping to break the parliamentary deadlock between the left, right and centrist parties that resulted from the summer election, as concerns for the fiscal situation in France remain unresolved. Meanwhile, in Germany, the government of Chancellor Olaf Scholz lost a no-confidence motion, which means new elections will take place in February.

The People’s Bank of China (PBoC) reduced two key interest rates, the one-year and five-year loan prime rates, each by 25bp to 3.1% and 3.6% respectively early in the quarter, which were slightly larger cuts than the market had been anticipating. Deflation in the housing market worsened further, with the house price index falling by - 6.0% year-on-year. Inflation remains low and declined slightly to 0.2% in November, as the pace of GDP growth slipped to 4.6% year-on-year in Q3 from 4.7% the previous quarter. The was some modestly positive labour market news, as the unemployment rate fell back to 5.0%. The Manufacturing PMI from the National Bureau of Statistics of China was largely unchanged over the period, ending at 50.1, though the services PMI surged to 52.2 in its latest release, surprising the market with its unexpected strength. The PBoC and Chinese authorities have been seeking to employ alternative means to stimulate growth, rather than through looser monetary policy, as bond yields have fallen to new lows in recent weeks.

In other emerging market countries, Brazil’s inflation rate continued to increase, reaching almost 5%, while the rate of industrial production increased to 5.8%. However, the central bank raised interest rates twice, by 150bp overall to 12.25%, partly in a bid to take some heat out of the economy and partly to help protect the currency and preserve currency reserves. The real reached a record low against a resurgent USD during the quarter, amid market concerns about the government’s stimulatory fiscal policies and their effects on debt levels. By contrast, Mexico’s inflation eased, with the core rate falling below 4% for the first time since 2021. That helped to allow the central bank to ease policy by 25bp to 10%. In India, inflation was volatile as food inflation was unpredictable. The Reserve Bank of India continued to maintain interest rates at 6.5%. Turkey’s inflation rate has been on an improving trend to below 50%. That has coincided with the central bank being able to ease interest rates, taking the policy rate to 47.5%, in the first move since March 2024. Elsewhere, Argentina’s inflation rate fell back to 166%, notably lower than its peak close to 300% in April.

Performance Review

The Fund outperformed the benchmark in the period. Active rates positioning underperformed, with overweights to the US and UK acting as the primary drag, while an underweight in Japanese government bonds contributed. Curve positioning slightly underperformed as well with a US steepener underperforming partially countered by a yen flattener. Cross-market trades outperformed with overweights to New Zealand and Australia against the US and an underweight Sweden against Germany, providing positive alpha. However, an underweight Canada versus the US and a long emerging markets against core rates underperformed. Inflation provided slight outperformance as well driven by an underweight European inflation against the US. Within Europe, an overweight to Spain against France provided outperformance.

Credit provided outperformance in the fourth quarter driven by an overweight to European investment grade against US investment grade and an overweight beta. An overweight to mortgage-backed securities (MBS) provided positive performance as well. Finally, foreign exchange outperformed, driven by underweights to the Canadian dollar, New Zealand dollar and Chinese yuan.

Market Outlook

We see a positive, though sub-trend growth environment, with inflation remaining above target and falling only slowly. The beneficial base effects helping to push inflation lower have faded, introducing a greater likelihood of higher headline rates in coming months. The new Trump administration could also bring an inflationary pulse in the US if it imposes meaningful tariffs on imports quickly or proceeds with large-scale deportation plans. However, in most areas (outside Japan) inflation data is not an impediment to easier monetary policy. In addition to the existing geopolitical turmoil, the new US administration brings uncertainty. Meanwhile, the political backdrop in the core of Europe is still overhanging markets, while in Asia, notably in South Korea, political turmoil is also a wildcard.

After a relatively strong year for US growth, compared to many other developed economies, we expect 2025 to deliver softer, though still positive growth of around 2.2%. Inflation is expected to trend lower, but progress is likely to be slow and it may remain volatile. Potential policy changes from the incoming administration create some uncertainty. Looser fiscal policy could provide a positive impetus to growth, while a mass deportation program could do the opposite. Widespread tariffs would likely push inflation higher though. The Fed is expected to remain on an easing tack, but policymakers have indicated a slower and more considered approach, as rates move slowly down toward a more neutral level. We see rates at 3.5% but the extent of cuts may be limited by upward risks to inflation forecasts, meaning they may not decline far below 4% in 2025. We see 10-year Treasury yields gravitating toward 4.10% in a year’s time.

Prospects for growth across the eurozone remain underwhelming, and any US imposition of tariffs on imports would likely present further challenges for the region. We do see growth improving, though only to around 1.3% in 2025. Positive real wage growth in 2025 could support consumption, but savings rates remain elevated, and the manufacturing sector has been under pressure for some time and remains weak. Consumer confidence has bounced as inflation has moderated but remains below the long-term average. Political risk remains a concern with the new French Prime Minister still attempting to achieve a stable government and Germany facing elections in February. We see inflation gradually falling back to its target level and the ECB being able to reduce rates further. Core/German bond yields are expected to decline slightly as 10-year yields move toward 2.10%.

In emerging markets, prospects for improved economic growth in China may continue, but could come under further pressure if the new US administration successfully imposes harsh tariffs on the country’s imports. We expect a slower trend to GDP growth in 2025, undershooting its 5% target again. Inflation is expected to gradually increase but we do not believe there is much of an upside risk to inflation, as is the case in much of the rest of the world. Across other emerging markets, the uncertainty surrounding forthcoming trade policy of the incoming US administration and recent USD strength and the associated potential for elevated currency volatility, has provided reasons for many central banks to pause and reflect before proceeding with more policy easing.

Investors should consider the investment objectives, risks, charges and expenses of a mutual fund carefully before investing. To obtain a prospectus, or a summary prospectus, if available, that contains this and other information about a fund, contact your financial professional or visit BNY Mellon Investment Management - Global Home - English. Read the prospectus carefully before investing. Past performance is no guarantee of future results.

Risks

Bonds are subject to interest-rate, credit, liquidity, call and market risks, to varying degrees. Generally, all other factors being equal, bond prices are inversely related to interest-rate changes and rate increases can cause price declines. Investing in foreign denominated and/or domiciled securities involves special risks, including changes in currency exchange rates, political, economic, and social instability, limited company information, differing auditing and legal standards, and less market liquidity. These risks generally are greater with emerging market countries. High yield bonds involve increased credit and liquidity risk than higher-rated bonds and are considered speculative in terms of the issuer's ability to pay interest and repay principal on a timely basis. The use of derivatives involves risks different from, or possibly greater than, the risks associated with investing directly in the underlying assets. Derivatives can be highly volatile, illiquid, and difficult to value and there is the risk that changes in the value of a derivative held by the portfolio will not correlate with the underlying instruments or the portfolio's other investments.

The Bloomberg Global Aggregate Index (Unhedged) is a flagship measure of global investment grade debt from twenty-four local currency markets. This multi-currency benchmark includes treasury, government-related, corporate and securitized fixed-rate bonds from both developed and emerging markets issuers. Investors cannot invest directly in any index.

Inflation is the rate of increase in the cost of living. Inflation is usually quoted as an annual percentage, comparing the average price this month with the same month a year earlier. Gross domestic product (GDP) is a monetary measure of the market value of all goods and services produced in a given period of time. The Consumer Price Index (CPI) is a measure that examines the weighted average of prices of a basket of consumer goods and services, such as transportation, food, and medical care. The Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) is an index of the prevailing direction of economic trends in the manufacturing and service sectors. Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) also known as consumer spending, is a measure of the spending on goods and services by people of the United States. The ZEW Economic Sentiment Index is a leading indicator of the economic outlook for the euro zone. It measures the confidence of financial experts on the economy over the next six months. A soft landing is a cyclical slowdown in economic growth that ends without a period of outright recession.

This material has been provided for informational purposes only and should not be construed as investment advice or a recommendation of any particular investment product, strategy, investment manager or account arrangement, and should not serve as a primary basis for investment decisions. Prospective investors should consult a legal, tax or financial professional in order to determine whether any investment product, strategy or service is appropriate for their particular circumstances. Views expressed are those of the author stated and do not reflect views of other managers or the firm overall. Views are current as of the date of this publication and subject to change. This information contains projections or other forward-looking statements regarding future events, targets or expectations, and is only current as of the date indicated. There is no assurance that such events or expectations will be achieved, and actual results may be significantly different from that shown here. The information is based on current market conditions, which will fluctuate and may be superseded by subsequent market events or for other reasons. References to specific securities, asset classes and financial markets are for illustrative purposes only and are not intended to be and should not be interpreted as recommendations.

Information contained herein has been obtained from sources believed to be reliable, but not guaranteed. No part of this material may be reproduced in any form, or referred to in any other publication, without express written permission.

Investment advisory services in North America are provided by Insight North America LLC, a registered investment adviser and regulated by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Insight North America LLC is associated with other global investment managers that also (individually and collectively) use the corporate brand Insight Investment and may be referred to as "Insight" or "Insight Investment." Insight is a subsidiary of The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation.

BNY Mellon Investment Adviser, Inc., Insight North America, LLC (the fund’s sub-adviser) and BNY Mellon Securities Corporation are companies of BNY. BNY is the corporate brand of The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation and may be used to reference the corporation as a whole and/or its various subsidiaries generally. © 2024 BNY Mellon Securities Corporation, distributor, 240 Greenwich Street, 9th Floor, New York, NY 10286.

data.title: Super Micro Computer: An Undervalued High-Growth Opportunity For Investors Willing To Accept Additional Risks
data.description:
Getting an opportunity to buy a well-known stock at a good value is quite a rare opportunity. This is especially true in a “hot” market environment like what we have today. In my view though, this opportunity exists for higher-risk investors in the form of Super Micro Computer, Inc. (NASDAQ:SMCI). Due to some financial reporting irregularities and a probe by regulatory agencies, its stock has fallen 83% from a high of $120 to a low of $20 back up to its current price of $48. This article will look at why this stock could be undervalued and deserves a closer look.

SMCI Story So Far

The business case and long-term trends for Supermicro still remain. The company is a leader in server infrastructure technology as it designs and manufactures a wide array of solutions for high-performance computing environments such as data centers. Its products include servers, storage systems, and racks. Its key differentiator is its Direct-Liquid Cooling (“DLC”) technology which is crucial for AI data centers. Management expects over 30% of new data centers will adopt this cooling technology over the next 12 months. I won’t dwell too much on a lengthy analysis of the business model as Supermicro is a well-known company and has been a hot stock this past year. However, I believe Supermicro, if it executes well, could be a key player in the AI revolution.

Supermicro recently released its preliminary Q2 2025 earnings, and it shows that the company has performed pretty well. These results are reflective of its ability to execute and quickly scale revenue. In fiscal Q2 2025 (ended December 31), Net Sales grew by roughly 54% to be in the range of $5.6 billion to $5.7 billion. Despite this growth, the company is expected to be profitable this quarter which is a rarity for these high-growth AI technology firms. Supermicro is expecting Non-GAAP diluted net income to grow by 5% year-over-year to a range of $0.58 to $0.60 per share or $339.6 million to $351.3 million. These results show that Supermicro is continuing to accelerate its growth plans and when combined with the stock trading somewhat cheaply should be interesting to investors.

A Chance to accumulate SMCI

Supermicro is currently trading at a discount compared to its historical figures due to the drop in its share price. Looking at the company’s Seeking Alpha quant ratings, the company has scored both an “A” and an “A-“ on both valuation and growth scores. Using management’s guidance of projected full-year sales of $23.5 billion to $25 billion and EPS of $0.58 to $0.60, I am able to calculate the individual price multiples for comparison. The reason I am using management guidance is discussed in the key risk section below.

I calculated that the company is trading at a Forward P/E ratio of between 19.9x and 20.6x after annualizing the quarter results. This multiple is lower than the Technology sector median of 33x. Looking at the Sales multiple which I prefer for fast-growing companies that are more focused on revenue as opposed to profitability, the P/S is between 1.12x and 1.19x. Supermicro’s ratios are much lower than the technology sector median of 3.3x.

In terms of an actual price target, I will plug in Supermicro’s most recent management guidance into my 3-stage high growth valuation model. As mentioned, Management expects fiscal 2025 full-year revenue to fall within the range of $23.5 billion to $25 billion. Their target for fiscal 2026 is much more ambitious at $40 billion in revenue close to a 60% growth in revenue. This is an extremely high level of growth, but I believe that management is justified due to the rapid growth of the AI server market.

According to research firm Research and Markets, the AI server market is estimated to grow at a CAGR of 34.3% reaching $837.83 billion by 2030. This is driven by increasing adoption by industries that are beginning to rely more heavily on AI. According to the latest data, Supermicro has roughly 9% market share with the ability to gain more, especially from ODM players as more complex higher performance servers gain in importance.

Therefore for my valuation model, I am assuming Supermicro’s revenues to grow in line with industry CAGR after the big revenue jump targeted by management in fiscal 2026. I believe that this is a realistic assumption given the historical growth rate of the company as well as the overall TAM of the industry. It should be noted however that valuation models are more “art” than “science” especially when trying to forecast revenues far into the future.

With regard to the company’s EBIT margins, Supermicro’s management has indicated that it expects both GAAP and non-GAAP gross margin is expected to be in the range of 11.8% to 11.9%. The entire AI server industry is undergoing a form of margin compression due to the intense competition and shortage of powerful AI semiconductors. However, it is my view that over the long term, profitability should eventually revert back to the industry average of 5.4% EBIT margin.

The discount rate I used was based on figures calculated by NYU’s legendary professor Aswath Damodoran for 2025. He calculated a cost of equity of 11.88% for tech companies. For the terminal value, I used the industry Price to Sales ratio of 3.31x for the Information technology industry. For the number of shares, I have grown the share count by 7.6% per year for the life of the model equivalent to the share count growth in 2024. I have also adjusted the number of shares assuming full conversion of the company’s convertible note more details of which are discussed below.

Running the valuation model gives me a target price of SMCI stock of about $211. This is well above the stock’s current price of $48. However, there is no such thing as a free lunch in the financial world and therefore Supermicro does come with some risks.

Key Risks and Mitigating Factors

So, even with all the positive catalysts, why is SMCI stock trading at such a discount? The elephant in the room is that the company is still working to finalize its most latest 10-K and 10-Q which it expects at the end of this month. The company is also dealing with controversy as it has received subpoenas from both the Department of Justice and the SEC. Therefore, it should be noted that the financial information I used on my model is based on management’s guidance therefore is preliminary and unaudited and may be subject to future revisions.

I believe though that management should be able to fix its financial reporting issues and that this was simply a result of the company growing at an astronomical pace. While delaying a filling is never fantastic news, in my view it's not the end of the world and companies can still bounce back. Major companies like Apple Inc. (AAPL) and Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) have had late fillings in the past. Therefore, I am hoping that the management of Supermicro will be able to sort this all out in quick order.

What gives me confidence regarding Supermicro’s future growth outlook and the strength of its current business is the fact that the company was able to raise additional capital. The company was able to secure $700 million in new 2.25% Convertible Senior Notes via a private placement (subject to customary closing conditions on February 20th). They were also able to amend the existing $1.725 billion Convertible Notes which will carry a 3.50% annual interest rate. While these notes were sold privately to “qualified buyers” they were placed by The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. (GS) and Evercore Group.

In my view these creditors must have done some level of due diligence on Supermicro’s financials and growth prospects as these notes do not have interest rates that signify that the company is distressed. In other words, if Supermicro is in danger of default the interest rates on these bonds would be much higher than the current US 3-year treasury yield of 4.3% as the market would place a high credit risk spread. Since the interest rates on these convertible notes are lower in my view this could signal that investors think SMCI stock has upside potential and that the company would be able to sort out its financial reporting issues in the near future. The issuance of these notes improves the liquidity situation of the company as it already had as of the end of 2024 total cash of approximately $1.4 billion.

Furthermore, by all indications, the company is continuing to execute its growth plans. Supermicro has recently announced the full production availability of its AI data center “Building Block Solutions” which utilizes NVIDIA Corporation's (NVDA) Blackwell platform. These new systems are specifically designed to manage complex AI-related tasks and have features like next-generation liquid-cooling and air-cooling technology and up to 12 systems with 96 NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs in a 52U rack. These advancements could cement Supermicro’s leadership position and ensure that it is a key player in the extremely lucrative AI server industry moving forward. In my view, I would prefer to focus more on the operations side and news like this makes me confident that management is on track to meet its growth potential.

Conclusion

To wrap this up, I believe that Supermicro presents a unique opportunity for investors willing to take on high risk. It's rare to see a fast-growing stock trading at a discounted price. Despite the key risks discussed and the negative sentiment the company faces due to financial reporting issues and regulatory scrutiny, I believe that the stock is still worth considering. This is especially true as the AI server market is set to experience rapid growth thus in my view the potential rewards outweigh the risks. I believe that the stock is a "Buy"

data.title: iA Financial Corporation Inc. 2024 Q4 - Results - Earnings Call Presentation
data.description: The following slide deck was published by iA Financial Corporation Inc. in conjunction with their 2024 Q4 earnings call.
